prior_claim_boundary: Live overlapping writers were blocked, but the model and runtime did not distinguish a live writer from a failed or exited process whose transient lock remained.

runtime_rules: A lock owned by a live process still blocks every overlapping claim.
A lock whose recorded owner process no longer exists is recovered under the atomic claim guard and emits stale_locks_recovered on the prior run.
A legacy lock without owner metadata is recoverable only when its verified final event is step_failed, locks_released, or closure_issued.
An idempotently resumed run reacquires or renews every declared lock before work continues.

field_lifecycle.legacy_absence_disposition: accepted only with verified terminal-event recovery; otherwise remains blocking
